metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
applicable: yes — CROSS-ADAPTER cell, recorded by run-cell-multi.sh.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
applicable: yes — CROSS-ADAPTER cell, recorded by run-cell-multi.sh.

## Reasoning

This scenario is about COEXISTENCE: one pi session and one DIFFERENT-adapter session (claudecode) running concurrently in the SAME project directory, each shown with its correct adapter label and fully independent state + metrics. This is NOT `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` (two instances of the SAME agent — a driver_gap for pi because the pi driver is single-session and cannot mint a per-slot second pi process). Here pi drives exactly ONE session next to a different adapter, so pi's single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep) is sufficient and the per-adapter PID-binding weakness that downgraded multiple-sessions-same-cwd does not apply — the partner is a different process name in a different transcript root.

### agent_supports: yes

Pi has no notion of, and imposes no constraint on, what other agent CLIs run in the same cwd. A `pi` process is an ordinary CLI REPL that materializes its own transcript under `~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/<ts>_<uuid>.jsonl` (see `core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/agent.go`, Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.pi/agent/sessions/}). Nothing about launching a second, unrelated agent (claude) in the same directory interferes with a running pi session. So the agent trivially "supports" coexistence — there is nothing for it to support or block.

### daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

The daemon labels each session by its adapter INDEPENDENTLY, by construction:

1. **Per-adapter process matching.** Pi registers `Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "pi"}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/agent.go`), while claudecode registers its own distinct matcher (~/.claude/projects watch + hook-based discovery). The process scanner runs every adapter's matcher separately (off the `allAgents` projections in `maps.go`), so a `pi` process is tagged `pi` and a `claude` process is tagged `claudecode` — the adapter label derives from which matcher hit, never from the cwd. Two different agents in one directory therefore get two correct, distinct labels.

2. **Disjoint transcript roots.** Pi's Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ".pi/agent/sessions"}` whereas claudecode watches `~/.claude/projects/`. The fswatcher roots are per-adapter (derived from the `allAgents` slice), so a pi transcript and a claude transcript never land in the same watch root and cannot be confused or merged, regardless of sharing a cwd.

3. **Per-(adapter, session_id) metrics + fresh parser per file.** Pi's Source instantiates a FRESH stateful `Parser{}` per transcript file (JSONLineParser{NewParser}), so the pi session never shares parser state with the claudecode session — a turn on the pi transcript drives only the pi session's state arc; the claudecode session's state is unaffected, and vice versa. There is no cwd-keyed merging — sessions are keyed by their own UUID under their own adapter.

The load-bearing PID-binding weakness from `multiple-sessions-same-cwd` (pi's DiscoverPIDByCWD cannot disambiguate two SAME-cwd SAME-adapter pi processes) does NOT bite here: the two coexisting processes have DIFFERENT process names (`pi` vs `claude`), so each adapter's pgrep-x matcher returns a disjoint candidate set and binds its own PID cleanly. Independent labeling/metrics fall out of the architecture rather than needing special-case code, the same way the codex/claudecode cross-adapter cell relies on it.

## Recording (2026-05-25)

Recorded by scripts/run-cell-multi.sh: it reads the scenario's cross_adapter[] list, launches the pi and claudecode interactive drivers CONCURRENTLY against ONE shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon (coexisting with production on an alternate port + IRRLICHT_HOME), then curates one per-adapter fixture each. The workspace events.jsonl (shared by the rows) shows the two-row independence above: a pi session and a claudecode session, each keyed to the same cwd slug, each with its own working->ready arc, no cross-contamination or merge. The pi cell stores the pi transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl + expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (both adapters). The scenario's requires:[headless_mode] gate is satisfied (capabilities.json → headless_mode: true).

The pi interactive driver needed a one-line ABI-parity change to honor $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (it previously hardcoded $STAGING/cwd), matching the claudecode/codex drivers — that lets both adapters share one workspace. This is the cross-adapter shared-cwd convention, NOT a new step type; pi still drives a single session with its existing send/wait_turn/sleep steps, so this is not a driver_gap.
caveats: Recorded by run-cell-multi.sh, which launches both adapters' drivers concurrently against one shared cwd under a single isolated --record daemon. The daemon observes claudecode via its transcript fswatcher (the isolated daemon is not on :7837 where claudecode's hooks POST), which suffices for this scenario's per-session working->ready arcs; pi is observed via its own ~/.pi/agent/sessions transcript fswatcher.

Distinct from multiple-sessions-same-cwd: that cell is applicable:false for pi (driver_gap — the single-session pi driver can't launch two concurrent SAME-cwd pi sessions, and DiscoverPIDByCWD can't disambiguate them). This cell pairs pi with a DIFFERENT adapter, so pi drives one session and the per-adapter independence holds.

pi mints its own session UUID (session_id_assignment:false), so — like codex and unlike claudecode — a recipe cannot pre-pin the session id; the spec asserts two DISTINCT sessions via new_session/same_session_as relations.

Every expected.jsonl phase anchors on a working transition, never a ready: pi's process scanner (ExactName{pi}, pgrep -x) emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready before the transcript is discovered; a ready-anchored new_session phase would greedily bind to that placeholder.

The pi interactive driver was given a one-line change to honor $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (cross-adapter shared-cwd convention, matching claudecode/codex). Not a new step type — pi still uses only send/wait_turn/sleep.

sources.note: Pi registers Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "pi"} (pgrep -x) and Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.pi/agent/sessions, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser}} — per-adapter matcher + disjoint watch root + fresh stateful parser per transcript file.
fswatcher roots, process scanners, and parser-factory map are all per-adapter projections of the allAgents slice — independent labeling/metrics across DIFFERENT adapters fall out of the wiring.

DiscoverPID = DiscoverPIDByCWD("pi", ...). The cwd-only narrowing weakness only bites two SAME-adapter pi processes in one cwd; here the partner is a DIFFERENT process name (claude), so each adapter's matcher returns a disjoint PID set and binds cleanly.

Cross-adapter recorder: reads cross_adapter[], launches each adapter's interactive driver concurrently against one shared cwd (IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD) under a single isolated --record daemon, then curates one per-adapter fixture each (shared workspace events.jsonl, per-adapter transcript).

Single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep/interrupt). Given a one-line change to honor $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD so it can share a workspace with a different adapter — the cross-adapter shared-cwd convention, not a new step type.
Sibling cross-adapter cell (codex paired with claudecode, yes/yes). Same architecture argument and same spec shape; the pi spec is adapted from it.
headless_mode:true (scenario requires it); session_id_assignment:false (pi mints its own UUID — recipe cannot pre-pin the id); subagents:false, permission_hooks:false.

preconditions: pi AND claude (and codex, the third concurrent adapter) CLIs on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording — run-cell-multi.sh isolated coexist mode (spawns its own --record daemon on an alternate port with its own IRRLICHT_HOME, so production keeps running)
no other pi/claudecode/codex session is live in the run cwd
setup: run-cell-multi.sh creates ONE shared cwd and launches each cross_adapter[] driver concurrently, all pointed at it via IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD (the pi interactive driver honors $IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_CWD so it shares the workspace with the other adapters)

pi registers Process.Match=ExactName{Name:pi} + Source=FilesUnderRoot{Dir:.pi/agent/sessions} with a fresh stateful Parser per transcript, disjoint from claudecode's ~/.claude/projects and codex's ~/.codex/sessions roots, so the daemon keys the pi session independently — its single-session driver (send/wait_turn/sleep) is sufficient because pi runs ONE session next to a DIFFERENT adapter (distinct from multiple-sessions-same-cwd, which needs two SAME-adapter pi sessions and is a driver_gap)
this cell stores the pi transcript for the replay golden; its events.jsonl and expected.jsonl span the whole workspace (every coexisting adapter)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: gamma

verify: events.jsonl contains the pi session and at least one different-adapter session, each keyed to the same cwd slug
each session has its own independent working->ready arc; no transition on one is attributed to the other (pi's fresh per-transcript parser guarantees no shared state)
no cross-contamination: the daemon never merges or flaps the pi row into another adapter's
